transcript_input: Patient says their lower back has been aching for 3 days after lifting boxes. Pain is a 6 out of 10 and goes into the right glute. During the exam, I found tightness in the QL and erectors. Straight leg raise was positive on the right at 45 degrees. I'm diagnosing this as a lumbar strain with trigger points. Today we did 60 minutes of myofascial release. Plan is 2 times a week for 3 weeks.

expected_output.subjective: Lower back pain (L4-L5) x3 days post-lifting. Pain 6/10, radiating to right glute. No paresthesia.
expected_output.objective: Hypertonicity in Erector Spinae and Quadratus Lumborum. Positive SLR (R) @ 45°.
expected_output.assessment: Lumbar strain with myofascial trigger points (ICD-10: M54.50).
expected_output.plan: 60min MFR and neuromuscular re-education (CPT: 97140, 97112). Frequency: 2x/week for 3 weeks.

transcript_input: Eight weeks post-op left rotator cuff repair. Patient feels pulling when reaching up. Sleep is bad. Pain is 4 at rest but jumps to 8 when moving. I see scar tissue on the anterior delt. Abduction is stuck at 90 degrees. Supraspinatus shows atrophy. This is adhesive capsulitis secondary to surgery. We did friction therapy. Goal is 120 degrees of abduction in 4 sessions.

expected_output.subjective: 8 weeks post-op L Rotator Cuff repair. 4/10 rest, 8/10 activity. Sleep disruption noted.
expected_output.objective: Scar tissue adhesions anterior deltoid. PROM abduction limited to 90°. Atrophy in supraspinatus.
expected_output.assessment: Adhesive capsulitis of left shoulder (ICD-10: M75.02).
expected_output.plan: Friction therapy and passive stretching. Goal: PROM abduction to 120° within 4 sessions.

transcript_input: Data entry clerk with wrist pain and tingling in the first two fingers. Happens after 4 hours of typing. Tinel's and Phalen's tests were both positive. Forearm flexors are super tight. Grip strength is down on the right side. Suspected carpal tunnel from repetitive work. I did deep tissue on the flexors and told them to fix their desk setup. Might need a PCP referral if it doesn't get better.

expected_output.subjective: Bilateral wrist pain with paresthesia (1st/2nd digits) associated with prolonged typing.
expected_output.objective: Positive Tinel’s/Phalen’s signs. Forearm flexor hypertonicity. Diminished grip strength (R).
expected_output.plan: Deep tissue to forearm musculature. Ergonomic education provided. Monitor for neuro progression; refer to PCP if no improvement.
